student_metadata.name: Piyush Kaushal
student_metadata.project_title: Learning SVM Classifiers with Indefinite Kernels

paper_metadata.title: Learning SVM Classifiers with Indefinite Kernels
paper_metadata.core_topic: Handling indefinite kernels in SVM optimization

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understanding the concept of indefinite kernels
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Explain what an indefinite kernel is and why it creates issues in standard SVM optimization.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped clarify the difference between positive semi-definite kernels and indefinite kernels and why convexity breaks.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I cross-checked the explanation with the paper's introduction and mathematical formulation.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Summarize how the paper modifies SVM learning to handle indefinite kernels.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided a simplified interpretation of the optimization adjustment proposed in the paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I re-read the algorithm section and checked the mathematical objective to confirm correctness.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Is this indefinite kernel SVM method feasible to reproduce on CPU using synthetic datasets?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped evaluate computational feasibility and understand complexity concerns.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I reviewed the algorithm steps and estimated computational requirements manually.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Why does standard SVM require the kernel matrix to be positive semi-definite?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Improved understanding of convex quadratic programming requirements.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I re-derived the dual SVM formulation from lecture notes.
